metadata.name: action_outcome_episodes
metadata.notes: Synthetic AIOps episodes designed for retrieval, action ranking, and recovery-outcome comparison.

policy_decision.reason: single-service rollback with known prior good version

rollback_plan.plan: redeploy checkout_42 if rollback worsens errors

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: p95 and error rate recovered within 10 minutes

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: checkout_42 increased payment pool contention

episodes.reflection: For checkout latency immediately after deploy with payment pool wait logs, rollback restored SLO faster than restart.

rollback_plan.plan: scale replacement pod and drain restarted pod

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: error rate remained above threshold

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: restart did not address deploy-level client pool regression

evidence_refs.description: restart did not change service-level errors

episodes.reflection: Pod restart is weak for checkout deploy regressions when the same timeout appears across replicas.

rollback_plan.plan: scale back to 8 replicas after SLO recovery

machine_outcome_label.label: partial_success
machine_outcome_label.notes: latency improved but stayed above target until later config fix

human_outcome_label.label: partial_success
human_outcome_label.notes: scale-out bought time but deploy still required follow-up patch

episodes.reflection: Scale-out is useful as mitigation for checkout CPU saturation but should not be treated as durable fix for deploy regressions.

policy_decision.reason: recent config has known previous value

rollback_plan.plan: reapply payment_pool_17 if revert increases payment errors

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: pool wait and p95 recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: config revert fixed bad pool size

episodes.reflection: When checkout latency follows a payment pool config change, config revert is safer and more direct than restart.

policy_decision.reason: payment route drain changes external dependency traffic

rollback_plan.plan: restore gateway A to 50 percent if gateway B saturates

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: timeouts dropped and success rate recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: external gateway A outage confirmed

episodes.reflection: Drain degraded payment gateway traffic when timeout traces isolate one external route.

rollback_plan.plan: scale back after 30 minutes

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: timeouts unchanged after scale-out

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: capacity was not the root cause; external gateway was timing out

evidence_refs.description: scale-out did not reduce external timeouts

episodes.reflection: Scale-out is ineffective for payment incidents when CPU is normal and traces point to external dependency timeout.

rollback_plan.plan: replace pod if restart loops

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: pod error skew disappeared

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: restart cleared corrupted TLS state

episodes.reflection: Restart is useful when payment errors are strongly skewed to one stateless pod.

policy_decision.reason: payment rollback affects checkout path but has known stable version

rollback_plan.plan: redeploy payment_31 after retry fix

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: timeouts and checkout errors recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: bad retry policy in payment_31

episodes.reflection: Rollback is the preferred action when payment timeouts align with a retry-policy deploy.

policy_decision.reason: recent config revert with prior good value

rollback_plan.plan: restore inventory_pool_9 if db overload increases

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: pool wait and p95 recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: bad pool config caused exhaustion

episodes.reflection: Config revert is strong when inventory pool exhaustion follows a pool-limit change.

rollback_plan.plan: scale down if db connections worsen

machine_outcome_label.label: rollback_required
machine_outcome_label.notes: db connection pressure worsened after scale-out

human_outcome_label.label: rollback_required
human_outcome_label.notes: scaling app replicas increased db connection contention

episodes.reflection: Do not scale out inventory when database pool is saturated; it can worsen connection contention.

logs: stale db connection on one pod

rollback_plan.plan: replace pod if restart loops

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: error skew disappeared

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: restart cleared stale db connections

evidence_refs.description: single pod stale db connection logs

episodes.reflection: Restart is appropriate for inventory db errors only when skewed to one pod, not for service-wide pool exhaustion.

rollback_plan.plan: resume bulk import after db connections recover

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: db saturation cleared after draining import writes

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: bulk import overwhelmed db pool

episodes.reflection: Traffic drain is useful for inventory db saturation caused by non-critical bulk writes.

rollback_plan.plan: allow cache refill from feature store

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: freshness lag cleared and CTR recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: cache namespace stale after flag rollout

episodes.reflection: Targeted cache purge works when recommendation freshness lag rises with high cache hit rate after a flag change.

rollback_plan.plan: replace pod if restart loops

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: freshness lag unchanged

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: problem was shared cache, not pod state

evidence_refs.description: restart did not clear cache staleness

episodes.reflection: Pod restart does not fix recommendation staleness when cache hit rate is high and freshness lag is shared.

rollback_plan.plan: redeploy v58 after fallback fix

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: error rate and CTR recovered

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: bad ranker deploy caused stale fallback

episodes.reflection: Rollback is better than cache purge when recommendation staleness coincides with ranker errors after deploy.

rollback_plan.plan: scale back after 30 minutes

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: freshness lag unchanged despite extra replicas

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: shared cache staleness was root cause

episodes.reflection: Scale-out is a poor response for recommendation cache staleness when CPU is low and freshness lag is high.

rollback_plan.plan: scale back after lag clears

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: lag dropped after scale-out

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: capacity was the main issue

episodes.reflection: Scale out order consumers when lag rises with low consumer error rate and traffic spike context.

policy_decision.reason: DLQ move affects one poison message and is auditable

rollback_plan.plan: requeue message after schema patch

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: consumer errors dropped and lag cleared

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: poison message caused retry loop

episodes.reflection: Queue reset with DLQ move works when backlog is driven by one poison message and high consumer errors.

rollback_plan.plan: stop restart and scale out if lag worsens

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: lag worsened during restart

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: restart reduced capacity during traffic spike

episodes.reflection: Do not restart healthy order consumers during capacity-driven backlog; it reduces throughput.

policy_decision.reason: shed noncritical async writes to protect checkout

rollback_plan.plan: restore noncritical events when broker throttle clears

machine_outcome_label.label: partial_success
machine_outcome_label.notes: checkout write latency recovered but queue lag remained high

human_outcome_label.label: partial_success
human_outcome_label.notes: traffic drain protected checkout but did not fix broker throttle

episodes.reflection: Traffic drain can protect checkout during broker throttle but should be paired with broker remediation.

policy_decision.reason: restart one replica while others serve traffic

rollback_plan.plan: drain traffic before retry if restart causes cold-cache latency

machine_outcome_label.label: partial_success
machine_outcome_label.notes: memory reset but cold cache kept latency elevated

human_outcome_label.label: partial_success
human_outcome_label.notes: restart mitigated memory leak but deploy fix was needed

episodes.reflection: Restart mitigates search memory leaks but may leave cold-cache latency; treat as temporary relief.

policy_decision.reason: regional search rollback but prior version known good

rollback_plan.plan: redeploy search_73 after leak fix

machine_outcome_label.label: success
machine_outcome_label.notes: rss and p95 stabilized after rollback

human_outcome_label.label: success
human_outcome_label.notes: search_73 introduced analyzer leak

episodes.reflection: Rollback is the durable fix for search memory leak when monotonic RSS growth starts after deploy.

rollback_plan.plan: scale back if memory/cost worsens

machine_outcome_label.label: failure
machine_outcome_label.notes: new replicas also leaked memory

human_outcome_label.label: failure
human_outcome_label.notes: scale-out did not fix code leak

episodes.reflection: Scale-out is a poor durable response for search memory leaks when every new replica repeats the leak.

rollback_plan.plan: restore traffic after shard rebuild

machine_outcome_label.label: partial_success
machine_outcome_label.notes: global latency recovered but shard still required rebuild

human_outcome_label.label: partial_success
human_outcome_label.notes: drain protected users while shard rebuilt

episodes.reflection: Traffic drain is useful for shard-isolated search latency but should be followed by shard rebuild or rollback.
